The booster target at the linac.
Time of flight with a flight path of 100 m.
The 270 liters Ne211 liquid scintillator tank.
Using the code REFIT, a least squares fitting program
1mm thick and served as monitor below 30 keV neutron
yield has been normalized firstly against the 1 keV
resonance in Fe-56 determined in an absolute
measurement with a Moxon-Rae detector and secondly
to the average capture cross-section of gold between
- finite sample effects, such as multiple scattering
All uncertainties are expressed as one
In addition there is an uncertainty in the
normalization of the data to the gold capture cross
and uncertainty in the flight path that gives an
additional error in the resonance energies of 0.01%
